facts: Cheque bounce under NI Act; demand payment of cheque amount with interest.
draft:
LEGAL NOTICE
UNDER SECTION 80 CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908

From:
Payee Name
Address: [Address]

To:
Drawer Name
Address: [Address]

Subject: Demand for payment of cheque amount - Cheque bounce

Sir/Madam,

A cheque bearing no. [number] dated [date] drawn on [bank] for Rs. [amount] was dishonoured. You are hereby called upon to pay the said amount along with interest within 15 days, failing which my client shall file a complaint under the Negotiable Instruments Act and initiate civil recovery.

Place:
Date:

Advocate for Payee

facts: Client's tenant has not paid rent for 6 months; demand payment and vacate premises.
draft:
LEGAL NOTICE
UNDER SECTION 80 CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908

From:
Landlord Name
Address: [Address]

To:
Tenant Name
Address: [Tenant Address]

Subject: Demand for arrears of rent and vacation of premises

Sir/Madam,

You are in occupation of the premises at [address] as tenant. You have failed to pay rent for the past six months. You are hereby called upon to pay the arrears of rent amounting to Rs. [amount] and vacate the premises within 30 days from the receipt of this notice, failing which my client shall initiate appropriate legal proceedings.

Place:
Date:

Advocate for Landlord

facts: Sale of movable machinery between two parties; payment in two instalments.
draft:
AGREEMENT FOR SALE OF MACHINERY

This Agreement is made on the ________ day of ________ at ________

BETWEEN:

1. Seller Name (Party of the First Part)
   Address: [Address]

2. Buyer Name (Party of the Second Part)
   Address: [Address]

WHEREAS the Seller agrees to sell and the Buyer agrees to purchase the machinery described as [description] for a total consideration of Rs. [amount].

NOW THEREFORE IT IS HEREBY AGREED AS FOLLOWS:

1. The Buyer shall pay 50% of the consideration at the time of execution and the balance within 30 days.
2. The Seller shall deliver the machinery in good working condition.
3. Risk and title shall pass to the Buyer upon full payment.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the parties have set their hands on the date first above written.

Seller				Buyer

facts: Loss of original documents; deponent seeking certified copy from authority.
draft:
IN THE COURT OF DISTRICT CONSUMER FORUM

AFFIDAVIT

I, Priya Singh, daughter of Vikram Singh, aged about 28 years, resident of [address], do hereby solemnly affirm and state as under:

1. That the original documents pertaining to [subject] have been lost.
2. That I am applying for certified copies from the concerned authority and this affidavit is in support of the same.
3. That the contents of the above affidavit are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief.

Deponent
Priya Singh

Place:
Date:

Verification: Verified at ________ on this ________ day of ________.

facts: Petition for quashing of FIR on grounds of settlement between parties.
draft:
IN THE HIGH COURT OF DELHI

CRIMINAL PETITION
Under Section 482 CrPC

Petitioner: Accused Name
Versus
Respondent: State

PETITION UNDER SECTION 482 CrPC

Most Respectfully Submitted:

1. That the petitioner has been arrayed as accused in FIR No. [number].
2. That the petitioner and the complainant have entered into a settlement and the complainant has no objection to quashing of the FIR.
3. That continuation of proceedings would be an abuse of process of law.

PRAYER

In view of the above, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to quash the aforesaid FIR and all proceedings emanating therefrom.

Place: Delhi
Date:

Petitioner

facts: Memorandum of Understanding for joint venture between two companies.
draft:
MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING

This MOU is entered into on ________ at ________

BETWEEN:

1. Company A (Party of the First Part)
   Registered Office: [Address]

2. Company B (Party of the Second Part)
   Registered Office: [Address]

WHEREAS both parties wish to explore a joint venture for [purpose].

NOW THEREFORE:

1. The parties shall maintain confidentiality of all shared information.
2. The parties shall negotiate in good faith to finalise a definitive agreement within 90 days.
3. This MOU is not legally binding except for clauses on confidentiality and good faith.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF the parties have executed this MOU.

Company A				Company B

facts: Deponent stating his age, residence and that he has not been convicted earlier.
draft:
IN THE COURT OF CIVIL JUDGE, DELHI

AFFIDAVIT

I, Suresh Mehta, son of Ramesh Mehta, aged about 35 years, resident of [address], do hereby solemnly affirm and state as under:

1. That I am the deponent and am well conversant with the facts deposed herein.
2. That I have never been convicted by any court of law.
3. That the contents of the above affidavit are true and correct to the best of my knowledge.

Deponent
Suresh Mehta

Place:
Date:

Verification: Verified at ________ on this ________ day of ________ that the contents of the above affidavit are true and correct.

facts: Client arrested under IPC 420, first offense, no prior record, family dependent on him.
draft:
IN THE COURT OF DISTRICT JUDGE, DELHI

BAIL APPLICATION
UNDER SECTION 439 CrPC

Applicant: Rajesh Kumar

Most Respectfully Submitted:

1. That the applicant has been falsely implicated in FIR No. 123/2024 under Section 420 IPC.
2. That the applicant is a first-time offender and has deep roots in society.
3. That the applicant undertakes to abide by all conditions imposed by this Hon'ble Court.

PRAYER

It is therefore prayed that this Hon'ble Court may kindly grant bail to the applicant on such terms and conditions as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit.

Place: Delhi
Date:

Applicant
Rajesh Kumar

facts: Accused in custody for IPC 406, dispute of civil nature, no flight risk.
draft:
IN THE COURT OF SESSIONS COURT, MUMBAI

BAIL APPLICATION
UNDER SECTION 439 CrPC

Applicant: Amit Sharma

Most Respectfully Submitted:

1. That the applicant is in judicial custody in connection with Crime No. 456/2024 under Section 406 IPC.
2. That the dispute is essentially of a civil nature and the applicant has cooperated with the investigation.
3. That the applicant has permanent residence and is not a flight risk.

PRAYER

It is therefore prayed that this Hon'ble Court may kindly grant bail to the applicant.

Place: Mumbai
Date:

Applicant
Amit Sharma
